sections.paragraph: i.q. שִׁית to set, to place. From this there twice occurs, pret. pl. שַׁתּוּ, Psa 49:15, כַּצּאֹן לִשְׁאֹל שַׁתּוּ “like sheep they place (them) in Hades,” i.e. they drive, thrust them down thither (compare Psa 88:5); Psa 73:9, שַׁתּוּ בַשָּׁמַיִם פִּיהֶם “they set their mouth against the heavens,” i.e. they assail heaven, and, as it were, provoke it, with proud and impious words.
